steps.title: Authenticate and set GCP project
commands: gcloud services enable run.googleapis.com cloudbuild.googleapis.com sqladmin.googleapis.com

steps.title: Create Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance
steps.notes: db-f1-micro is ~$7-10/mo. Upgrade to db-g1-small if you need more headroom.

steps.title: Get Cloud SQL connection name
steps.notes: Returns something like rdf-api-project:europe-central2:rdf-postgres. You need this for step 5.

steps.title: Generate a JWT secret
steps.notes: Save this value securely. You will use it in the deploy command.

steps.title: Deploy to Cloud Run
commands: gcloud run deploy rdf-api --source . --region europe-central2 --allow-unauthenticated --port 8000 --memory 1Gi --cpu 1 --min-instances 0 --max-instances 3 --add-cloudsql-instances <CONNECTION_NAME_FROM_STEP_3> --set-env-vars DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:<PASSWORD_FROM_STEP_2>@/rdf?host=/cloudsql/<CONNECTION_NAME_FROM_STEP_3> --set-env-vars JWT_SECRET=<SECRET_FROM_STEP_4> --set-env-vars WORKERS=2 --set-env-vars LOG_LEVEL=INFO --set-env-vars CORS_ORIGINS=["https://rentgen.kraus.uk"]
steps.notes: Cloud Run builds the container from your Dockerfile via Cloud Build. First deploy takes 2-3 minutes. The --allow-unauthenticated flag makes the API publicly accessible.

steps.title: Get the Cloud Run service URL
commands: gcloud run services describe rdf-api --region europe-central2 --format='value(status.url)'
steps.notes: Returns something like https://rdf-api-xxxxx-ew.a.run.app. This is your API base URL for the frontend.

steps.title: Initialize database schema
steps.notes: The app auto-creates tables on startup via lifespan hooks (scraper_db.connect, prediction_db.connect, krs_repo.connect). Just hitting /health triggers this. If you need to seed the admin user, run the seed script locally pointing at the Cloud SQL instance.

steps.title: Configure your frontend
instructions: In your frontend, set the API base URL to the Cloud Run service URL from step 6.
All API calls should use this base, e.g.: POST https://rdf-api-xxxxx-ew.a.run.app/api/podmiot/lookup
If using auth endpoints, store the JWT token from /api/auth/login and send it as Authorization: Bearer <token>.

steps.title: Tighten CORS for production
commands: gcloud run services update rdf-api --region europe-central2 --set-env-vars CORS_ORIGINS=["https://rentgen.kraus.uk"]
steps.notes: Replace with your actual frontend domain. You can add multiple origins as a JSON array.

cost_estimate.cloud_run: Free tier covers 2M requests/mo. Beyond that ~$0.40/million requests + CPU/memory charges only while handling requests.

useful_commands.view_logs: gcloud run services logs read rdf-api --region europe-central2 --limit 50
useful_commands.update_env_var: gcloud run services update rdf-api --region europe-central2 --set-env-vars KEY=VALUE
useful_commands.check_status: gcloud run services describe rdf-api --region europe-central2
